message:
feat(theme): add custom theme loading from JSON files

Implement comprehensive custom theme system allowing users to define
their own color palettes via JSON configuration files. Themes are
validated for required fields, color format, and contrast ratios.

CustomTheme structure:
- Primary colors: primary, secondary
- Semantic colors: success, warning, error, info
- Text colors: text, text_muted, text_inverse
- Background colors: bg, bg_subtle, bg_highlight
- Border colors: border, border_focus

Validation features:
- Required field presence checking
- Hex color format validation (#RGB or #RRGGBB)
- Contrast ratio warnings for accessibility
- Graceful fallback to default theme on errors

Demo showcase enhancements:
- Jobs page improvements with enhanced job tracking display
- Updated mouse E2E tests for new interaction patterns
- Help overlay snapshot updates

Also syncs beads issue tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2tl1","title":"demo_showcase core: Program options + terminal lifecycle","description":"Ensure the demo configures bubbletea runtime options intentionally.

Implementation notes:
- Drive behavior from `demo_showcase::Config` (single source of truth), not scattered env reads.

In main/program bootstrap:
- alternate screen on by default (Config override: `--no-alt-screen`)
- focus reporting enabled
- bracketed paste enabled
- mouse enabled by toggle (Config + in-app Settings)
- set window title

Also ensure:
- graceful exit leaves terminal restored
- handle Ctrl+C and QuitMsg cleanly

Acceptance:
- Repeated runs never leave the terminal in a broken raw mode.
- `--no-alt-screen` produces a usable experience and allows demonstrating bubbletea `println/printf`.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:41.147935817Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:01:17.393130573Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:01:17.393066163Z","close_reason":"Implemented Program options: focus reporting, mouse support from Config, window title. All 144 tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2tl1","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:28:39.779293987Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2tl1","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:41.161925295Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2tl1","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:13.369647978Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ucj","title":"demo_showcase UX: visual design system + theme presets","description":"Define the visual language so the app feels like one product.

Specify:
- Spacing scale (padding/margins), border styles, rounded vs sharp rules.
- Color semantics (primary/success/warn/error/muted/border) mapped to lipgloss ColorSlots.
- Typography/weight usage (bold/faint), emphasis rules.
- Theme presets to ship (e.g., Dracula, Nord, Catppuccin Mocha, Light, ASCII/NoColor) + naming.

Acceptance:
- Concrete rules that can be encoded as shared style helpers and reused everywhere.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:06.814237235Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:33:44.210289534Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:33:44.210207271Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","lipgloss","theme","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ucj","depends_on_id":"bd-33mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:02.290972241Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ucj","depends_on_id":"bd-3kcq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:06.837012355Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2xlv","title":"demo_showcase theme: load custom theme from file + contrast checks","description":"Optional but compelling showcase: load a custom theme from disk and validate it.

Why:
- It proves the theming system isn’t hard-coded and can be user-configured.
- Contrast validation demonstrates a mature design approach (readability matters).

Requirements:
- CLI/Config:
  - support `--theme-file <path>` (takes precedence over preset theme)
- File format:
  - pick a single stable format (recommend: TOML or JSON) and document the schema
  - map file values into semantic tokens (not raw ad-hoc styling)
- Validation:
  - validate required fields are present
  - run contrast checks for key pairs (fg/bg, muted/bg, selection/bg)
  - surface warnings in About/Diagnostics and/or as a toast on load
- Failure handling:
  - invalid file should not crash; fall back to a safe preset and show a clear error

Testability:
- Add unit tests for:
  - parsing valid theme files
  - rejecting invalid/missing fields
  - contrast warning behavior

Acceptance:
- Demonstrates lipgloss theme IO + validation in a real workflow.
- The app remains readable and stable even with bad user themes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:27.588370671Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:31:46.886871699Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","lipgloss"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xlv","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:28:43.964560626Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xlv","depends_on_id":"bd-39zu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:27.601173399Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xlv","depends_on_id":"bd-3mh2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:40.699647818Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2xzk","title":"huh: Form Component Bug Fixes & Robustness","description":"# Epic: huh Form Component Bug Fixes & Robustness

## Background
The huh crate provides interactive form components (Select, MultiSelect, Text, FilePicker).
Deep review identified several bugs and edge cases that affect reliability.

## Issues Identified

### 1. Select Field Missing Empty Guard - FIXED ✓
**Location**: Line 1864 (was missing guard, now fixed)
**Status**: Fixed in this review session

### 2. MultiSelect Cursor Position with Filtering - HIGH
**Location**: Lines 2231, 2185-2198
**Problem**: When filtering is applied, cursor position calculation uses wrong index space.
`self.cursor` indexes unfiltered list but calculation assumes filtered list indices.
**Impact**: Cursor appears at wrong position after filtering.

### 3. Select Field Incomplete Filter Support - MEDIUM
**Location**: Lines 1610, 1815-1875
**Problem**: Select has filter_value field and calls filtered_options(), but navigation
logic doesn't respect filtered state. Navigation may jump to non-visible items.
**Impact**: Poor UX when filtering - navigation broken.

### 4. FilePicker Single-File Edge Case - MEDIUM
**Location**: Lines 3660-3671
**Problem**: With single file, navigation condition prevents selection.
`if self.selected_index < self.files.len().saturating_sub(1)` is always false
when len=1 (0 < 0 is false).
**Impact**: Single-file pickers non-functional.

### 5. Form Initialization Repeated - LOW
**Location**: Lines 4621-4628
**Problem**: init_fields() called on every update while state is Normal and
current_group is 0. Could cause repeated initialization.
**Impact**: Minor performance, potential state corruption.

## Design Philosophy
- All edge cases should be explicitly handled
- Empty/single-item states should work correctly
- Filter state should be fully integrated with navigation
- Defensive programming throughout

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All identified bugs fixed with tests
- [ ] Edge cases for 0, 1, and many items all work
- [ ] Filtering properly integrated with navigation
- [ ] No regression in existing functionality","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:19:12.348259552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:41:09.230039458Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:41:09.229971211Z","close_reason":"All children complete: bd-17p2 (MultiSelect cursor, CLOSED), bd-8wrz (Select filter, CLOSED by WildSnow), bd-1isw (FilePicker single-file, CLOSED by GoldPeak). huh form component bug fixes and robustness hardening is done.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug-fixes","forms","huh"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xzk","depends_on_id":"bd-gyzr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:19:12.365634836Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2zeq","title":"demo_showcase: quality gates (tests/docs/perf)","description":"Ensure demo_showcase is reliable and maintainable.

Deliverables:
- Tests for core update logic (use bubbletea simulator where it helps).
- A short in-repo run guide + keybindings (README section).
- Performance sanity (no obvious O(n^2) redraws; avoid huge allocations).

Acceptance:
- `cargo test -p demo_showcase` meaningful; `cargo run -p demo_showcase` never leaves terminal broken.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:11.546258289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:11.560105442Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","quality"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zeq","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:11.560065147Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2zj3","title":"E2E Tests: demo_showcase navigation flows","description":"End-to-end tests for demo_showcase navigation and page flows.

## Purpose
Test complete user workflows through demo_showcase,
verifying the app works as a real user would experience it.

## Test Requirements

### Application Startup
- [ ] App starts and renders initial dashboard
- [ ] Sidebar shows all pages
- [ ] Status bar displays correctly
- [ ] Help overlay accessible via '?'

### Page Navigation
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts 1-8 navigate pages
- [ ] Sidebar selection works
- [ ] Page state persists during navigation
- [ ] Back navigation if implemented

### Each Page Smoke Test
- [ ] Dashboard - metrics load and display
- [ ] Services - service list renders
- [ ] Jobs - job list with progress
- [ ] Logs - log entries display, filter works
- [ ] Docs - documentation tree navigable
- [ ] Files - file browser works
- [ ] Wizard - can start wizard flow
- [ ] Settings - settings toggle correctly

## Logging Requirements
- [ ] Log every key press with timestamp
- [ ] Log expected vs actual page after nav
- [ ] Capture terminal state on failure
- [ ] Include timing information

## Success Criteria
- All navigation paths tested
- Each page renders without error
- No dead-end states","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldPeak","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:27.855435034Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:47:24.353840429Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:47:24.353771681Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zj3","depends_on_id":"bd-1cgb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:27.878940499Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2zj3","depends_on_id":"bd-28kk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:03.377359642Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2zj3","depends_on_id":"bd-t1aq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:38.911195389Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3m5y","title":"Unit Tests: lipgloss Style composition","description":"Unit tests for lipgloss style composition and rendering.

## Current State
lipgloss has basic style tests but needs:
- Complete style inheritance testing
- All style property combinations
- ANSI output verification
- Unicode width handling

## Test Requirements

### Style Properties
- [ ] foreground/background colors (named, hex, ANSI256, RGB)
- [ ] bold/italic/underline/strikethrough
- [ ] padding (all directions)
- [ ] margin (all directions)
- [ ] border (all styles)
- [ ] width/height/max-width/max-height
- [ ] align (left, center, right)

### Style Composition
- [ ] Style::inherit() copies properties
- [ ] Style::copy() creates independent copy
- [ ] Property override precedence
- [ ] Unset vs default values

### Rendering
- [ ] Style::render() produces correct ANSI
- [ ] Multi-line content handling
- [ ] Padding with borders
- [ ] Margin with alignment
- [ ] Width truncation and padding

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Empty string rendering
- [ ] Very long lines
- [ ] Unicode characters (emojis, CJK)
- [ ] Nested ANSI sequences in content

## Success Criteria
- Every style property has dedicated tests
- Composition rules are fully tested
- ANSI output matches expected sequences","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenCastle","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:49.043249336Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:44:29.683451059Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:44:29.683383904Z","close_reason":"Added 100+ comprehensive unit tests for lipgloss Style composition. Tests cover: text attributes (bold/italic/underline/strikethrough/reverse/blink/faint), colors (hex/ANSI256/RGB/adaptive), dimensions (width/height/max-width/max-height), alignment, padding, margin, borders (all styles), style composition (clone/override), all unset methods, and rendering edge cases (empty strings, multiline, unicode, inline mode, nested ANSI).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lipgloss","testing","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3m5y","depends_on_id":"bd-28co","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:49.072015568Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3mh2","title":"demo_showcase theme: semantic tokens + shared style helpers","description":"Build the styling foundation for the entire app.

Implement:
- A theme module that maps lipgloss `ThemeContext` (ColorSlots) into semantic UI primitives.
- Shared helpers: `card()`, `panel()`, `badge()`, `button()`, `table_style()`, `modal()`, `status_bar()`, etc.

Guidelines:
- No ad-hoc hex strings scattered across pages.
- Prefer semantic roles (primary/success/warn/error/muted) and allow theme switching.

Acceptance:
- A page can be built using only shared style helpers + minimal page-specific overrides.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:23.252512028Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:37:33.002106933Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:37:33.002041060Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","lipgloss","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mh2","depends_on_id":"bd-2ucj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:32.937693109Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mh2","depends_on_id":"bd-39zu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:23.270859361Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mh2","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:31.724599289Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3mtt","title":"demo_showcase core: command palette (search + actions)","description":"Optional but very compelling polish feature: a command palette ("quick actions") that makes the app feel modern.

Why:
- It dramatically improves discoverability and speed for power users.
- It showcases composing bubbles components (textinput + list) into a cohesive UX.

UX:
- Trigger:
  - `/` (or another agreed key) opens the palette
  - `esc` closes
- Layout:
  - text input at top
  - filtered list of commands below
- Behavior:
  - fuzzy-ish matching over command names + keywords
  - enter runs selected action
  - shows short descriptions and shortcuts (optional)

Actions to include (minimum):
- Navigation:
  - jump to Dashboard/Jobs/Docs/Wizard/Files/Logs/Settings
- Toggles:
  - toggle theme picker / cycle theme
  - toggle no-color / reduce-motion
- Utilities:
  - export current view (plain/HTML)
  - open diagnostics (pager / export)

Implementation guidance:
- Maintain a small registry of commands (id, title, keywords, handler).
- Handlers should emit bubbletea Cmds / messages (no direct side effects in UI code).

Acceptance:
- Palette is keyboard-friendly, fast, and feels integrated (not a bolted-on demo).
- Actions are safe in headless/self-check mode (no hangs).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:02.480166189Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:32:37.827452925Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mtt","depends_on_id":"bd-14v4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:26.750440223Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mtt","depends_on_id":"bd-1aad","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:28.035096998Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mtt","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:02.499012063Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3mxk","title":"Jobs: progress visualization polish","description":"Polish job progress visualization so it looks like a modern CI dashboard (compelling, readable, information-dense).

Why:
- Progress UI is where “toy” demos instantly reveal themselves.
- This task should make the Jobs page feel premium even in a plain terminal.

Requirements:
- Use `bubbles::progress` for determinate progress:
  - clear fill, percentage (optional), and label
  - themed colors that match status (running/success/fail/cancel)
- Use spinners for indeterminate states:
  - e.g., “waiting”, “queued”, “uploading”, “finalizing”
- Status clarity:
  - icons/chips for state
  - duration + ETA-ish hint when possible
  - differentiate “stuck” vs “slow” (e.g., warn color after threshold)
- Visual polish:
  - consistent spacing and alignment across rows
  - good behavior in narrow widths (truncate/wrap rules)
  - respects `--no-color` (still readable) and `--no-animations`

Optional (nice if it’s easy):
- Stage-based progress (build/test/deploy) with per-stage completion markers.

Acceptance:
- Looks like a real CI dashboard.
- Clear at a glance: what’s running, what’s blocked, what failed, what finished.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - rendering decisions given job state (spinner vs bar, colors/labels)
  - width-constrained rendering produces stable output
- E2E:
  - wizard -> jobs scenario should assert at least one running job shows an updating progress indicator.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:23.217085325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:43:57.767439291Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","jobs","progress"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mxk","depends_on_id":"bd-1dr6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:23.232965048Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mxk","depends_on_id":"bd-3rwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:56.844461854Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mxk","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:59.582343606Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Why:
- Progress UI is where “toy” demos instantly reveal themselves.

Acceptance:

 {"id":"bd-3nit","title":"E2E Tests: demo_showcase wizard workflow","description":"End-to-end tests for demo_showcase deployment wizard workflow.

## Purpose
Test the complete multi-step wizard deployment flow,
including validation, navigation, and completion.

## Test Requirements

### Wizard Flow
- [ ] Start wizard from page 7
- [ ] Step 1: Service name entry and validation
- [ ] Step 2: Service type selection
- [ ] Step 3: Environment selection
- [ ] Step 4: Environment variables
- [ ] Step 5: Review and confirmation
- [ ] Deployment progress display
- [ ] Completion notification

### Validation
- [ ] Empty service name rejected
- [ ] Invalid characters rejected
- [ ] Required selections enforced
- [ ] Clear error messages displayed

### Navigation
- [ ] Next/previous step buttons work
- [ ] Can go back and modify earlier steps
- [ ] Cancel abandons wizard
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts work

### Integration
- [ ] Deployment triggers job creation
- [ ] Job visible in Jobs page
- [ ] Logs generated in Logs page
- [ ] Dashboard metrics update

## Logging Requirements
- [ ] Log each wizard step transition
- [ ] Log validation attempts/results
- [ ] Log deployment progress events
- [ ] Full form state at submission

## Success Criteria
- Complete wizard flow works E2E
- Validation prevents bad input

 {"id":"bd-3nyf","title":"EPIC: Integration Test Coverage","description":"Integration tests that verify cross-crate behavior and real terminal interaction.

## Philosophy
- Test how crates work together
- Test actual terminal I/O where possible
- Verify ANSI escape sequence correctness
- Test across different terminal capabilities

## Key Integration Points
1. bubbletea + bubbles - components in event loop
2. bubbletea + lipgloss - styled output rendering
3. glamour + lipgloss - markdown with styles
4. huh + bubbletea - form in TUI context
5. charmed_log + any - logging integration

## Test Infrastructure Needs
- Terminal emulator/PTY test harness
- ANSI sequence capture and verification
- Cross-platform terminal capability detection
- Timing-sensitive test handling","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:41.653513760Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:41.663266853Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nyf","depends_on_id":"bd-665u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:41.663223292Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-4d5e","title":"E2E scenario: smoke tour (visit every page, no panics)","description":"Scenario: a deterministic smoke tour that proves the app boots, routes, and renders across the whole surface area.

Steps (headless/custom-IO):
- Start app with deterministic Config (fixed seed, animations off).
- Navigate to:
  - Dashboard
  - Jobs
  - Docs
  - Wizard
  - Files
  - Logs
  - Settings
- Open/close the help overlay.
- In Settings:
  - toggle no-color/ASCII mode and confirm ANSI escapes disappear from output
  - switch theme preset and confirm the visible theme indicator updates
- Quit cleanly.

Optional/conditional steps (skip if feature not present yet):
- Open command palette.
- Start guided tour mode.

Assertions:
- No panics and no hangs.
- App state transitions as expected (page headers match).
- Key UI strings appear in output (title, page headers, theme name label).

Acceptance:
- Runs fast and deterministically.
- Catches regressions in routing/layout/theme plumbing early.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:35.237974495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:01:15.574324685Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:01:15.574255606Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-1aad","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:23.194963017Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-1nwu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:20.869742832Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-28l3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:13.866362166Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-2q4a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:07:51.168707692Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:08.439038707Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:12.230376916Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-3rwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:10.963093037Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-fdf3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:16.132478669Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:09.690902812Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-mwty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:18.554874272Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:35.267810523Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-5hs1","title":"Wish: fix 28 unused_async clippy warnings in tests","description":"The wish/tests/e2e/ test files have 28 clippy::unused_async warnings that prevent cargo clippy --workspace -D warnings from passing cleanly.

Scope:
- Review each async function in crates/wish/tests/e2e/ and tests/common/mod.rs
- Either add await points where appropriate or remove async keyword
- Ensure tests still pass after changes
- Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings to verify fix","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:43:23.590032477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T16:50:12.350469495Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T16:50:12.350395978Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5hs1","depends_on_id":"bd-212m.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:43:23.596927485Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-665u","title":"EPIC: Comprehensive Test Coverage Suite","description":"Master epic for achieving comprehensive test coverage across all charmed_rust crates.

## Goals
- Real tests only - NO mocks, fakes, or stubs
- Direct integration with actual terminal/TTY behavior
- Property-based testing for algorithmic/numeric code
- E2E tests with detailed logging for debugging

## Sub-Epics
1. Unit Test Coverage - expand tests in every crate
2. Integration Test Coverage - cross-crate and terminal tests
3. E2E Test Coverage - full application workflows
4. Property Test Coverage - proptest for edge cases

## Success Criteria
- All public APIs have at least one test
- Integration tests verify cross-crate behavior
- E2E tests cover critical user workflows
- Property tests catch edge cases in numeric/algorithmic code","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:17.362248783Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:58:24.179013006Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":22,"issue_id":"bd-665u","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ARCHITECTURE NOTE: This test coverage effort is structured in 4 parallel tracks:

1. **Unit Tests (bd-28co)** - Foundation layer, can start immediately
   - Tests individual functions/methods in isolation
   - NO mocks - use real implementations
   - Priority: Start with bubbletea and lipgloss (most used)

2. **Integration Tests (bd-3nyf)** - Depends on unit tests
   - Tests cross-crate interactions
   - Includes terminal I/O verification
   - Requires PTY test harness

3. **E2E Tests (bd-1cgb)** - Depends on unit + infrastructure
   - Tests complete user workflows
   - Requires logging framework first (bd-28kk)
   - Focus on demo_showcase application

4. **Property Tests (bd-ndn1)** - Parallel with unit tests
   - Uses proptest for random input generation
   - Focus on numeric/algorithmic code
   - Lower priority but high value for edge cases

RECOMMENDED EXECUTION ORDER:
1. Build E2E logging infrastructure (bd-28kk) - unblocks all E2E work
2. Start unit tests for core crates (bubbletea, lipgloss)
3. Add integration tests as unit tests complete
4. Run E2E tests on demo_showcase
5. Add property tests for regression prevention","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:58:24Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-6wkl","title":"E2E scenario: mouse scroll + click-to-select","description":"Scenario: validate mouse scroll + click-to-select/focus behavior.

Why:
- Mouse support is a premium feature and easy to regress.
- This scenario proves bubbletea mouse plumbing + demo_showcase hit-testing/mapping is correct in a headless, deterministic way.

Steps (suggested):
- Start the app with mouse enabled.
- Navigate to a scrollable screen (Docs viewport or Files preview).
- Inject mouse wheel events to scroll down, then up.
- Navigate to a clickable/selection surface (Dashboard card grid or Jobs table).
- Inject a mouse click at known coordinates and assert selection/focus changes.
- (Optional) Toggle mouse off in Settings and assert wheel/click no longer changes state.

Assertions:
- Scroll wheel changes viewport offset (rendered output differs and/or a scroll indicator updates).
- Click selects the intended item (visible highlight/focus state changes deterministically).
- No panics; no runaway input loops.

Implementation notes:
- Prefer the E2E runner’s semantic DSL events (`MouseMsg`) for stability.
- If needed, add a “raw byte” mode sub-test that injects an xterm SGR mouse sequence to exercise the InputParser.

Acceptance:
- Catches regressions in mouse mapping and makes failures actionable (step id + last frame + artifact path).","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:50:58.031885548Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:25:20.790400307Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","mouse","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:17.148362656Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-3d1w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:21.100671155Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:19.756678314Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-3ps4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:18.467597486Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:50:58.052269553Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- Mouse support is a premium feature and easy to regress.

Steps (suggested):

- Inject mouse wheel events to scroll down, then up.
- Navigate to a clickable/selection surface (Dashboard card grid or Jobs table).

- (Optional) Toggle mouse off in Settings and assert wheel/click no longer changes state.

- Click selects the intended item (visible highlight/focus state changes deterministically).
- No panics; no runaway input loops.

 {"id":"bd-7iul","title":"demo_showcase core: demonstrate bubbletea println/printf","description":"Use bubbletea `println`/`printf` in a controlled way.

 {"id":"bd-7jxu","title":"demo_showcase theme: polished UI components (tabs, chips, banners)","description":"Build higher-level styled UI primitives so pages can be assembled like a design system.

Why:
- The demo needs a cohesive, premium visual language.
- Centralizing these patterns prevents ad-hoc styling and keeps pages consistent.

Components to provide (examples):
- Tabs/pills for sub-sections
- Chips/tags for status (ok/warn/error/running)
- Alert banners (info/warn/error) with icons and optional actions
- Empty states (icon + title + guidance)
- Progress-with-label layouts
- Small "stat" widgets (label + value + delta)

Design constraints:
- Must work in:
  - truecolor and 256-color
  - no-color/ASCII mode (fallback styles)
  - narrow terminals (components truncate gracefully)
- Components should be usable from any page with minimal configuration.

Testability:
- Components should be snapshot-testable (render a small representative example set).

Acceptance:
- Pages can assemble these like a cohesive design system.
- UI feels consistent and premium across Dashboard/Jobs/Logs/Docs/Wizard.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:25.396250192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:46:10.479515924Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:46:10.479451744Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","lipgloss"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7jxu","depends_on_id":"bd-39zu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:25.411568884Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-7jxu","depends_on_id":"bd-3mh2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:34.170348451Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
